questions.question: Given an expression string s, examine whether the pairs and orders of {, }, (, ), [, ] are correct.
questions.solution: Using stack to ensure every opening has a matching closing.

questions.question: Given an array of intervals, merge all overlapping intervals and return the array having only non-overlapping intervals.
questions.solution: Merge overlapping intervals after sorting and comparing.

rounds.tips: Discussing about the project works for most of the time and the technical questions were asked at the last 15 minutes. Prepare accordingly.
Discussing about the project works for most of the time and the technical questions were asked at the last 15 minutes. Prepare accordingly.

key_tips: Know every detail of the projects mentioned in the resume.
mistakes_to_avoid: Don't hesitate to ask doubts that are valid.

questions.question: Given an array, output the cumulative sum (indirectly specified in the problem).
questions.solution: Input: [1,2,5,6] → Output: 26 (sum of all prefix sums)

questions.question: Based on the Prefix and Suffix Max Array.
questions.solution: Build prefix and suffix arrays and simultaneously traverse both.

rounds.tips: No behavioral questions.

questions.question: Given energy levels, decay rates, and a minimum threshold for magical stones, return the maximum time the shield will survive.
questions.solution: Logical-based approach.

questions.question: Given a grid with unblocked paths, blocked paths, and traps, return the shortest path to the destination.
questions.solution: Slight variation of Dijkstra's algorithm to accommodate traps.

rounds.tips: No behavioral questions. Only self-intro is asked.

key_tips: Focus on explaining the solution instead of directly coding. Make sure the interviewer understands your approach before you start coding.
mistakes_to_avoid: Try to be open-minded with the interviewer and explain all the approaches you thought for the particular problem.

questions.question: MCQ
String manipulation
Find triplets satisfying a custom condition

rounds.tips: Strong understanding of Computer fundamentals.

questions.question: Delete a node with given value in a singly linked list; follow-up on memory management and memory leaks.
Given a circular linked list, delete every kth node until a single node remains.
Lifecycle of objects in heap.
questions.solution: Discussed young generation (eden, survivor spaces) and old generation, and types of GC.

questions.question: Multithreading in Java.
questions.solution: Threads via Runnable/Thread class, race condition, synchronized, notify/wait, Semaphore, ExecutorService, CompletableFuture.

rounds.tips: Explain approach on whiteboard; think aloud; write code on paper without IDE; consider edge cases; be strong in one language and know its internals.

questions.question: Group Anagrams - standard DSA question; focus on edge cases.
System Design: Design a video streaming platform for students and professors (professors upload, students stream in parallel) in a cost-effective way.
questions.solution: Client, API gateway with rate limiting, microservice architecture, block storage, caching, CDN, metadata storage, Docker, Kubernetes, Prometheus, Grafana.

questions.question: Open a project repo from GitHub (Real-Time Monitoring System) and explain every feature and discuss extensibility for new features.
Computer Networks: TCP, UDP, Routing, DNS, what happens when you type google.com.
questions.solution: Relate everything with practical application and real-time usage of different protocols.

rounds.tips: Be ready for everything; stay calm; do real-world projects; give possible related answers.

questions.question: Brief introduction, relocation, experience, higher studies plan.

rounds.tips: Don't tell them your higher studies plan. Give polished answers.

mistakes_to_avoid: Having superficial understanding of something and having that on the resume.

questions.question: 2 medium level coding questions (C/C++); MCQs on OS, C, Microprocessor, Aptitude, COA.

rounds.tips: Be strong in basics of C, important aptitude topics, OS scheduling.

questions.question: Remove Linked List Elements - remove nodes with Node.val == val.
Reverse Nodes in K-Group.
Sum of Boundary Traversal of a binary tree (return sum of boundary nodes).
Implement Queue using Stack.

rounds.tips: Be strong in DSA - linked list and binary tree.

questions.question: How does the 'ls' command work; virtual memory and paging; memory hierarchy; cache levels; compilation of C/Java/Python; int/float storage in C; bubble sort for linked list; TCP layers; TCP vs UDP differences; why video conferencing doesn't use TCP.

rounds.tips: Have basic knowledge of COA, CN, and a strong foundation in C.

questions.question: Self introduction; relocation preference discussion (Bangalore, Hyderabad, Delhi).

rounds.tips: Be truthful.

key_tips: Be confident on yourself, stay calm and positive.
mistakes_to_avoid: Tell whatever approach comes to your mind, it may be right or wrong; don't hesitate.

questions.question: Rearrange elements of an array to maximize the number of indices where rearranged[i] > original[i].
questions.solution: Brute-force approach (sorting + visited[] arrays); passed 11/15 testcases.

questions.question: Place k buildings in a grid (with obstacles) to minimize the maximum distance from any empty cell to the nearest building.
questions.solution: Passed 6/15 testcases.

questions.question: MCQ questions.
questions.solution: Spend less time on MCQs; study using YouTube one-shot videos.

rounds.tips: Spend more time on coding questions. Prepare for MCQs using YouTube one-shot videos.

questions.question: Temperature conversion (Celsius to Fahrenheit) in any programming language.
questions.solution: f = (9/5)*c + 32 (solved in Java)

questions.question: Find the number of vowels in a string.
questions.solution: Convert string to lowercase, check each character against a,e,i,o,u, increment count.

rounds.tips: Be confident; questions asked from DSA, OOPs, DBMS, GitHub commands. Add only what you know on your resume.

questions.question: Sort an array of n numbers.
questions.solution: Bubble sort O(n^2) → merge sort (divide and conquer) O(nlogn).

rounds.tips: Be confident and speak clearly. Know your resume top to bottom. Questions based on projects; 1 coding question on sorting.

questions.question: Questions about family, academics, and real-life scenario questions. No technical questions.

rounds.tips: Be honest; provide relevant answers.

key_tips: Stay consistent and persistent. Don't overthink placements. Be honest during online assessments and interviews.
mistakes_to_avoid: Binge watching anime/web series. Scrolling reels. Having less sleep.
experience_narrative: Results came after 28 days. Got shortlisted for Josh Technology, Zoho, American Megatrends, Harts India, ExxonMobil during the wait. It was the first time getting to the 2nd round.

resources_used: Github Repos for System Design & LLD

questions.question: Compress a character array using run-length encoding in constant extra space.
questions.solution: Loop with count to construct the answer.

questions.question: Find minimum edit distance between two strings (Edit Distance).
questions.solution: Levenshtein distance DP approach.

rounds.tips: Had Aptitude and Technical MCQs. Be strong in CS Fundamentals, Basic Aptitude, DP, and String Problems.

questions.question: Deep copy a linked list with random pointers.
questions.solution: HashMap for brute force; then copy nodes next to originals and traverse for optimization.

questions.question: Find pairs that sum to a target in a sorted doubly linked list.
questions.solution: HashMap brute force; optimized with two pointers.

questions.question: Design a High Level Transaction API for a Bank.
Create a table for a Movie Ticket Booking System and normalize to BCNF.
OS questions: Multithreading, Multiprocessing vs Multitasking, Daemon Threads.

rounds.tips: Be familiar with CS Fundamentals (OS, DBMS). Come up with brute force first, then optimize. Approach is more important than completely solving the problems.

questions.question: Design a data structure for a Contacts Application and Master Call Log using OOP concepts.

rounds.tips: Be familiar with OOP concepts. Correctly identify classes and methods. Use correct data structures and explain your reasoning. Java OOP is preferred over Python.

questions.question: Puzzle: n boxes; up to k boxes are poison-free and poisoned thereafter. Find the poisoned box in minimum operations.
SQL queries and explanation of resume projects.

rounds.tips: Be familiar with puzzles, SQL, and your projects.

questions.question: Self-Intro, Family Background, Hackathons experience, Leetcode & CodeForces discussion, Projects.

rounds.tips: Be authentic and express yourself clearly.

key_tips: Be confident. Approach will be given more importance. Be technically strong in core CS Fundamentals.
mistakes_to_avoid: Don't be quiet if you are stuck. Ask for hints and ask questions if you have any doubts.
experience_narrative: Was asked difficult technical questions that really tested my understanding. Being able to solve them gave a strong sense of confidence and motivation.

questions.question: Describe a project from coursework/internship; draw architecture diagram and explain design approaches and resource management.
questions.solution: Used STAR approach (Situation, Task, Action, Result).

questions.question: Write an algorithm to print a diagonal anti-pattern of numbers in a 5x5 grid.
questions.solution: Basic looping solution.

questions.question: Puzzle: Two candles, each burning for 1 hour (unevenly). Measure 1 hour and 30 minutes.
questions.solution: Light one candle from both ends (burns in 30 min); the other burns for 1 hour.

rounds.tips: Be confident, explain your thoughts, take time to think, and don't rush.

questions.question: Given an array, remove all duplicate elements and return a new array with only unique values.
Given two trees, identify whether they are identical.
Explain the differences between a process and a thread.
Write an assembly code version for the Collatz sequence.

rounds.tips: Learn basics of 8086 Assembly Language, DSA.

questions.question: Bit Manipulation
String Manipulation

rounds.tips: Learn basics in C/C++.

key_tips: Be strong in your basics. Repeat and rinse the topics you've learned.
mistakes_to_avoid: Don't rush before getting a clear understanding of the problem. Don't always try for an optimal solution before the brute approach.

rounds.tips: Time management is essential. Stay calm. Attempt easier questions first. Avoid random answers.

questions.question: Given a list of device names, if a name repeats, append a number (starting from 1) to make each unique.
Count the number of triplets (subsequences of size 3) whose sum is divisible by d.
Two SQL questions (Easy-Medium).

rounds.tips: Cover basics of SQL and practice easy to medium-level array problems. Start with brute-force first.

questions.question: System Design GD on 'User Authorization Controls' - enhancing from functionality-level access to region, department, and reporting-structure-based controls.

rounds.tips: Be clear with your ideas. They expect your approach, not the exact answer. Gain insights from teammates.

questions.question: Given an array, determine if it is possible to rearrange elements such that no two identical elements are adjacent.
Find the length of the longest substring with no repeating characters.

rounds.tips: Focus on approach and manual tracing. Interviewer also asked self-introduction, project explanation, and follow-up questions from the GD.

questions.question: Internship projects and experience, reason for not getting PPO, traits followed, changes to implement if joining Birla Pivot.

rounds.tips: Don't be stressed. Be honest. Be confident and bold in making your point.

key_tips: Be clear with your points before delivering them.
mistakes_to_avoid: If unsure about something, admit it openly while showing readiness to learn.

rounds.tips: Time management is crucial. Focus on accuracy over quantity.

questions.question: Given a list of device names, if a name repeats, append a number to make it unique.
Swap adjacent digit pairs in a string number if both are odd or even; return the highest possible number.
SQL: Find total requests for each available product, ordered by request count descending, then product name ascending.
SQL: For each user, return total, min, and max transaction amount in March, ordered by email.

rounds.tips: Review Arrays, Strings, HashMap, HashSet, and basic SQL (JOINs, Aggregates, GROUP BY, ORDER BY).

questions.question: Multi-elevator management: enhance algorithm to manage two elevators in tandem.
questions.solution: Assign floor requests dynamically using priority score based on direction, distance, waiting users, and available space. Data structure: Priority Queue.

rounds.tips: GD is collaborative. Identify challenges, listen actively, and build on teammates' points.

questions.question: How did you choose the ML models in your intern project?
Explain flow of Online Auction System project; tables used; why bid table is needed; relationship between auction and bid table.
Big Billion Sale scenario: how to manage high traffic before the sale starts?
questions.solution: Load Balancing + Caching frequently accessed products.

questions.question: What is the use of a gateway?
questions.solution: Single entry point for clients; handles load balancing and authentication.

questions.question: How is a password stored? How do you compare it?
questions.solution: Stored as hashed values; compare by hashing the input and comparing with stored hash.

questions.question: Sort an array of integers in descending order of frequency without duplicates. Time and space complexity?
questions.solution: HashMap for frequency; PriorityQueue with custom comparator. Time: O(nlogn), Space: O(n).

rounds.tips: Research the company. Connect skills/projects to the company's domain. Know all projects thoroughly. Revise DSA and DBMS.

questions.question: About internship experience; balancing internship and academics; GD solution discussion.
Suggestions on process; defending ideas; GD self-rating; strengths; focus beyond academics; 2-3 year vision at Birla.

rounds.tips: Show enthusiasm. Be interactive with the HR.

mistakes_to_avoid: Spending too much time on a single question.
Not clarifying questions when confused.

resources_used: One shot videos for revising CS Domain Concepts

questions.question: Validate Sudoku - check whether it's a valid Sudoku (smaller range, 3x3 or 4x4).
Validate Nonogram - check if the number of blacks in a matrix's row and column are satisfied.

rounds.tips: Be thorough with DSA and know all complexities and when to use a particular data structure.

questions.question: Tell me about yourself; resume-based questions; explain most complex/favorite project; frontend screens; database design and retrieval; GET, PUT, POST; favorite data structures and complexities; behavioral questions; brief discussion on Swing.
Pascal's Triangle.

rounds.tips: Be thorough with your resume. Be confident and honest.

key_tips: Resume matters a lot. Be very clear with what you write.
Prepare a concise 'Tell me about yourself'.
Practice DSA in a language other than Python if you're in third year.
mistakes_to_avoid: Don't add anything to the resume other than what you know.
experience_narrative: The overall experience was easier compared to other companies since it is a Financial company.

rounds.tips: Focus on CS core subjects (OOPs, Cloud Computing, CSS, JS) and aptitude questions.

questions.question: Present the hackathon project; discuss feasible alternative solutions. Evaluated on uniqueness, innovation, and completeness.

rounds.round_feedback: Best presentation; the panel liked the flow and unique solution.
rounds.tips: Articulate your solution clearly; focus on a unique solution; don't add too much content in PPT, mention keywords and explain while presenting.

questions.question: Difference between pandas and numpy; explain a project and why you chose that tech stack.
Behavioral: What if a client asks you to complete the project before the earlier mentioned deadline?
questions.sample_answer: Analyze the work needed and new deadline; if possible, agree; otherwise, explain to the client why it's not technically feasible without compromising quality.

rounds.round_feedback: Appreciated technical skills and fluent English.
rounds.tips: Be clear about CS core subjects; prepare for your chosen domain concepts; mostly questions from chosen domain, resume, behavioral, and situational.

key_tips: Explore previous year hackathon problem statements.
mistakes_to_avoid: Don't go for naive or common AI solutions.
Discuss all possible solutions before finalizing.
Don't jump into implementation without discussion.
Split implementation according to team members' skill sets.

rounds.tips: Be strong in your core concepts. Basic verbal and aptitude skills also required.

questions.question: Is EV a solution for pollution?
Strategy vs execution.

rounds.tips: Be expressive and inclusive. Active listening and diversified thinking needed.
Questions mainly focused on projects; deeper knowledge expected in core domain based on role.

key_tips: Skill-based shortlist is followed; good technical and behavioral skills help pass through rounds.
mistakes_to_avoid: Don't lose your confidence.
Resume plays an important role; don't mess up your portfolio.
Elaborate your portfolio; no need for a single-page resume.
experience_narrative: Experienced a new way of selection process.

rounds.tips: Prepare basic conceptual questions from Python, HTML, JS, OS, and logical reasoning.

questions.question: 24-hour hackathon: given a problem statement, submit a full solution for judges to verify.
questions.solution: Full Stack Development + Machine Learning.

rounds.tips: Build a fully integrated solution. Clearly understand the problem statement. At least one standout feature beyond specified requirements is a must.

questions.question: Questions about an ML/DL project from resume.
Given a scenario, which ML model would you choose and why?
Coding: Longest Common Subsequence.
DB: Write SQL query for the given problem (includes GROUP BY and DISTINCT).

rounds.tips: Striver's sheet, LeetCode, OS, and DBMS concepts.

key_tips: Be consistent in solving LeetCode problems. Be thorough with most concepts. Understand the methodology for each question.
mistakes_to_avoid: Be thorough with your resume; questions are asked only from the resume. Don't overthink. No bluffing.

rounds.tips: Revise Computer Networks thoroughly (OSI & TCP/IP layers, protocols, subnetting, routing, congestion control). Always start with the easy coding problem. Write clean and efficient code. Manage time wisely.

Covered OS, DBMS, Computer Networks, DSA, and OOPs with theory and coding. Expect situation-based questions. Aim for simple and correct solutions. Always think aloud. Ask for clarification if needed. Prepare strong self-introduction.
Know your projects inside-out. Revise OS, DBMS, and DSA. Expect general questions on programming language choices or club experiences.
Short and simple. Questions about location preferences, stipend details, and basic information.

key_tips: Clarify politely if you don't understand a question.
mistakes_to_avoid: Don't jump straight into coding without thinking.
Avoid generic behavioral answers.
Don't forget to ask questions at the end.
Don't stay silent if you don't understand.
Avoid rambling.
Don't underestimate confidence and clear communication.
Don't ignore edge cases.
Don't write messy or unstructured code.

questions.question: Array Manipulation

rounds.tips: Be clear with your projects. Learn more than just programming languages (Microservice Architecture, DevOps, etc.).
Be curious and put up a good attitude. Citi specifically expects candidates to be aware of how Citi operates, its core values, and how it makes money.

mistakes_to_avoid: Don't lose the opportunity to ask the interviewer questions.

rounds.tips: Practice using YouTube videos. Write formulas near problems. Revise regularly. Practice speed. Learn topic by topic.

questions.question: Question about SAP. Candidate explained SAP full form and general purpose as ERP, then highlighted their own database experience (MongoDB, Neo4j).
Explain Artificial Intelligence and related projects.

rounds.tips: Interviewer assessed communication skills, role-based knowledge, and ability to apply theoretical knowledge to practical applications.

key_tips: Prepare introduction well (concise, structured, and relevant).
mistakes_to_avoid: Not revising formulas or key shortcuts before the test.
Focusing only on certain topics.
Poor time management across sections.
Getting stuck on unfamiliar problems instead of moving on.
Ignoring previous mistakes from practice sessions.

rounds.tips: Pattern: 90 mins, Language Skills + General Aptitude (Reasoning + Quantitative) + CS Fundamentals with 30 MCQs + 2 coding questions (arrays and strings, Easy to Medium). Practice previous year OA from GFG, use Indiabix for aptitude, focus on LeetCode medium-level problems. Strengthen two-pointer and other patterns.

questions.question: OOPs: basics, objects, classes, inheritance, polymorphism.
SDLC: Agile and Waterfall models.
Testing: Black-box testing.
Coding: Java program to count occurrences of characters in a string.
Behavioral: Are you a team player? Where do you see yourself in 5 years? Why Deloitte?

rounds.tips: Brush up CS fundamentals (OOPs, SDLC, testing). Practice popular arrays & strings coding questions. Prepare behavioral answers. If from AIML background, review LLMs, RAG, and core AI concepts. Communicate clearly.

key_tips: Ask a sensible question when interviewer asks 'do you have any questions?'
mistakes_to_avoid: Don't say something you don't know well; instead, acknowledge the gap and say you will look it up. Don't waste the interviewer's time.

rounds.tips: Try to answer maximum number of questions. Complete and submit as soon as possible. Use aptitude formulae cheatsheets.

questions.question: Case study regarding an automotive company; discuss solution in a panel of 8 students.

rounds.tips: Be confident and clearly communicate thoughts. Language is very important. Interaction with panel members is evaluated.

questions.question: DBMS and SQL queries; Python and C code writing; questions about resume projects.

rounds.tips: Be thorough in SQL. Explain your approach when writing code.
Research about the company before the interview. Know everything in your resume in detail. Questions were also asked about the company and why you want to join them.

key_tips: Previous year interview experiences are useful.
Practice LeetCode problems by topic (arrays, DP, strings) to differentiate patterns.
mistakes_to_avoid: If something is unclear, ask and clarify. Avoid assuming on your own.
Never forget to ask questions at the end.

questions.question: MCQ

rounds.tips: Refer Careerride for aptitude, GeeksForGeeks for CS core.

questions.question: Reverse a string.
questions.solution: Used two pointers and while loop.

questions.question: What is inheritance?
What is polymorphism?
Write code for multiple inheritance and multi-level inheritance.
Introduce yourself.

rounds.tips: Be thorough in pillars of OOPs and basic data structure topics like arrays, strings, and two pointers.

mistakes_to_avoid: Avoid referring only to YouTube videos.
experience_narrative: Interview was well-rounded, covering technical concepts, project discussions, problem-solving, behavioral, and technical questions.
